Title: A secreted fungal laccase targets the receptor kinase OsSRF3 to inhibit OsBAK1–OsSRF3-mediated immunity in rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Secreted fungal laccase; copper-containing polyphenol oxidase; three Cu-oxidase Pfam motifs; signal peptide
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secreted; translocated into host cell; nucleus; plasma membrane; cytoplasm
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in rice spikelets/protoplasts; immune suppression assayed after chitin treatment and pathogen infection
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter; HIGS RNAi construct in pDS1301
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to fungal virulence; toxic to plants and animals and threatens human health
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rice false smut balls on infected spikelets/panicles
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ustilaginoidin accumulation and fungal virulence; heterologous expression in rice increases susceptibility and suppresses PTI; HIGS against UgsL reduces ustilaginoidins in smut balls and enhances resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UgsL is a secreted laccase effector required for U. virens vegetative growth, laccase activity, ustilaginoidin biosynthesis, and full virulence. 2. Heterologous expression of UgsL in rice suppresses PTI outputs and increases susceptibility to U. virens, M. oryzae, and Xoo. 3. UgsL interacts with OsSRF3, promotes its ubiquitin-26S proteasome-dependent degradation, and interferes with OsBAK1-mediated phosphorylation of OsSRF3. 4. Host-induced gene silencing of UgsL in rice lowers fungal UgsL transcripts and decreases ustilaginoidin content in smut balls.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. virens infection; chitin elicitation (8 nM); exogenous ustilaginoidin application during infection; MG132 treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion and complementation in U. virens; catalytic-site mutagenesis; HIGS in rice; heterologous overexpression in rice; RT-qPCR; laccase activity assay; HPLC for ustilaginoidins; metabolomics (LC-MS); Y2H; pull-down; Co-IP; LCI; BiFC; degradation and ubiquitination assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty reported for heterologous UgsL-expressing rice in the absence of pathogen challenge; promotes disease susceptibility
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dual-function protein: biosynthetic enzyme in ustilaginoidin pathway and virulence effector in host cells; signal peptide is important for virulence and also affects ustilaginoidin biosynthesis; laccase activity is required for OsSRF3 degradation-promoting function
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong HIGS target for reducing rice false smut severity and lowering ustilaginoidins/mycotoxin burden in grain production systems
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted metabolomics, CNSA: CNP0005733; small RNA-seq for HIGS-derived siRNAs, accession NA

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: STRUBBELIG receptor family LRR-RLK; signal peptide; SUB domain; six LRRs; transmembrane domain; proline-rich region; cytoplasmic kinase domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript levels markedly induced after U. virens infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher during pathogen infection; overexpression lines show enhanced PTI
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Defense-associated metabolic reprogramming and PTI output including ROS production and phenylpropanoid-related defense gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive signaling regulation through PTI activation; protein stability controlled by phosphorylation and ubiquitin-26S proteasome-mediated degradation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsPR1b; OsPR10a; OsRBOHA; OsPAL1; MAPK cascade
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PTI-associated gene expression and promotes ROS burst, MAPK activation, and callose deposition
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Chitin/PAMP perception; OsBAK1-mediated phosphorylation; antagonized by fungal effector UgsL
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms/interacts in OsBAK1–OsSRF3 plasma membrane immune complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates broad-spectrum disease resistance and PTI in rice; overexpression enhances resistance without growth or yield penalty; knockout increases susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Decisive positive immune regulator and valuable resistance gene resource; broad-spectrum controller of pathogen resistance without adverse agronomic cost
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSRF3 physically interacts with UgsL and OsBAK1 at the plasma membrane. 2. Knockout of OsSRF3 increases susceptibility to U. virens, M. oryzae, Xoo, and R. solani, whereas overexpression enhances resistance. 3. OsSRF3 activates PTI outputs including ROS burst, MAPK activation, callose deposition, and defense gene expression. 4. UgsL promotes OsSRF3 ubiquitination/degradation, while OsBAK1-mediated phosphorylation stabilizes OsSRF3.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. virens infection; M. oryzae infection; Xoo infection; R. solani infection; chitin elicitation (8 nM); MG132 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; complementation with phosphomimic variants; pathogen inoculation assays; ROS/DAB/callose assays; RT-qPCR; Y2H; pull-down; Co-IP; LCI; BiFC; cell-free degradation assay; ubiquitination assay; in vitro and in vivo phosphorylation assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression enhanced resistance without adverse effects on plant growth or yield
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ser670 phosphorylation is particularly important for stability and immune function; OsSRF3 degradation requires plant-derived ubiquitin-proteasome machinery
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent candidate for breeding or engineering broad-spectrum disease resistance with low apparent fitness cost; phospho-stabilized alleles may further improve resistance
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native edited locus via CRISPR/Cas9; overexpression in pU1301-Flag

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression lines analyzed for resistance and OsSRF3 phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PTI signaling upstream of defense-associated metabolic outputs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly promotes PTI-associated outputs through phosphorylation/stabilization of OsSRF3
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: PAMP perception during pathogen infection; antagonized indirectly by UgsL competition for OsSRF3 binding
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms OsBAK1–OsSRF3 immune complex at plasma membrane
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates rice resistance to U. virens and is required for full OsSRF3-mediated immunity; overexpression enhances resistance but causes growth defects
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key upstream positive controller of OsSRF3-mediated PTI and disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsBAK1 physically interacts with OsSRF3 in vitro and in vivo. 2. OsBAK1 directly phosphorylates OsSRF3, mainly at Thr661 and Ser670. 3. Phosphorylation, especially at Ser670, promotes OsSRF3 stability and immune function. 4. Silencing OsBAK1 compromises OsSRF3-mediated resistance and PTI responses.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. virens infection; M. oryzae infection; chitin elicitation (8 nM)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and RNAi knockdown in rice; pathogen assays; pull-down; Co-IP; LCI; BiFC; in vitro kinase assay; phospho-tag gel; phosphosite LC-MS; protoplast phosphorylation assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: UgsL does not interact with OsBAK1 directly but competes with OsBAK1 for OsSRF3 binding and reduces OsBAK1-dependent phosphorylation of OsSRF3
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful immunity enhancer, but deployment may require moderated expression or allele engineering because constitutive overexpression causes growth defects
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression in pU1301-Flag; RNAi knockdown in SRF3-OE background
